skill.name: face-swap
skill.description: Swap a face or character into video or images on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes across community Wan 2-2 Animate, GPT Image 2 Edit, Nano Banana Edit, Flux Kontext, and Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro. Picks the right model based on user intent: still image vs video, single vs batch, photoreal vs stylized.
skill.summary: AI-powered face and character swapping for images and video using the RunComfy CLI with automatic model selection.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 129 patterns across 321 lines of SKILL.md. All findings are false positives from Markdown documentation: backtick patterns are inline code formatting and code blocks, not executable shell commands. All URLs are documentation links to runcomfy.com or example placeholder domains (your-cdn.example). Filesystem references document CLI tool behavior, not skill-level filesystem access. No malicious code, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. The real risks are inherent to CLI wrapper skills: external command execution (scoped to runcomfy only), network calls to third-party API, and dual-use face-swap technology. The skill includes responsible-use guidelines with consent requirements and refusal instructions.

medium_findings.title: User content sent to third-party API
medium_findings.description: User-provided image, video, and audio URLs are transmitted to RunComfy API endpoints (model-api.runcomfy.net) for AI processing. This includes potentially sensitive biometric facial data. Users should be aware their media leaves their environment. The skill documents this behavior but does not provide local processing alternatives.

low_findings.title: Dual-use deepfake technology
low_findings.description: The skill facilitates face-swapping which can create misleading or harmful synthetic media. The skill includes consent guidelines and agent refusal instructions in its Security and Privacy section, but these are advisory and not programmatically enforced. The responsibility for ethical use rests entirely with the operator.

low_findings.title: Allowed-tools wildcard permits any runcomfy subcommand
low_findings.description: The skill frontmatter declares allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *). The wildcard means any runcomfy subcommand is permitted. While scoped to a single CLI tool, new subcommands added to runcomfy in the future would be automatically allowed without re-audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command execution via CLI wrapper
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to execute runcomfy CLI commands via Bash. All commands use the runcomfy binary with structured JSON input arguments. No shell injection surface exists since input is passed as JSON strings via --input flag. The CLI does not shell-expand user content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network calls to external API endpoints
dangerous_patterns.description: The runcomfy CLI makes HTTPS requests to model-api.runcomfy.net, runcomfy.net, and runcomfy.com. These are the documented RunComfy service endpoints. Input images and output files are transferred over these connections. No other external domains are referenced.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem access for token and output storage
dangerous_patterns.description: The runcomfy CLI stores authentication tokens at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600 and writes generated output files to user-specified directories via --output-dir. This is standard CLI behavior documented in the skill, not direct filesystem access by the skill itself.

content.user_title: Swap Faces in Images and Videos with AI
content.value_statement: Replace faces in images and videos using AI models on RunComfy. The skill automatically selects the best model for your use case, from single still-image swaps to full video character replacement.
actual_capabilities: Route face-swap requests to the best AI model based on input type and user intent
Swap faces in still images with high fidelity using GPT Image 2 Edit, Nano Banana Edit, or Flux Kontext
Replace characters in videos with audio-driven animation via Wan 2-2 Animate
Transfer motion from a reference performance onto a target character with Kling Motion Control
Process batch identity swaps across up to 20 frames consistently
Guide users through consent and disclosure requirements for synthetic media
limitations: Requires the runcomfy CLI to be installed and authenticated with a RunComfy account
All processing happens on RunComfy cloud infrastructure; does not work offline
Output quality depends on the underlying AI model and reference image quality
Cannot programmatically enforce consent; ethical use relies on operator judgment

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Production
use_cases.description: Replace a product model with a brand ambassador face across an entire campaign image set using batch processing.

use_cases.title: Video Post-Production
use_cases.description: Swap an actor face in existing footage for localization or character replacement while preserving the original audio and scene.

use_cases.title: Creative Character Design
use_cases.description: Test different character designs by swapping faces onto reference images quickly without manual photo editing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic face swap on a still photo
prompt_templates.prompt: Swap the face in this image with [target person name or reference]. Use the best model for still images.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a photo and want to replace the face with someone else using a reference.

prompt_templates.title: Video character replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the character in this video with [new identity]. Preserve the original audio, lighting, and background.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to swap a character in a video clip while keeping the scene intact.

prompt_templates.title: Batch identity swap across multiple images
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the same face from the first image to all other images in this set. Keep lighting, pose, and background consistent across all outputs.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have multiple product shots and need the same model face in all of them.

prompt_templates.title: Motion transfer with character swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the motion and performance from [source video] and apply it to [target character image]. The target character should perform the exact same movements with their own appearance.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to transfer a dance or action performance to a different character.

output_examples.input: Swap the CEO face onto the spokesperson in our company overview video.
output_examples.output: I will use Wan 2-2 Animate for this video face swap. Please share the CEO portrait image and the company overview video. The model will produce a new video with the CEO face on the spokesperson while preserving the original audio and scene composition.

output_examples.input: Put my face on all 10 product images for the online store catalog.
output_examples.output: I will use Nano Banana Edit for batch processing. Please share your face photo as the identity reference and the 10 product images. The model will swap your face into all images while keeping the product settings, lighting, and composition unchanged.

output_examples.input: Change only the model face in this photo to a woman in her 30s with high cheekbones. I do not have a reference photo.
output_examples.output: I will use Flux Kontext Pro since you do not have a reference image. This model can edit the face based on your description while preserving pose, clothing, hair, lighting, and background exactly. Please share the photo you want to edit.

best_practices: Always confirm the user has rights to both the target face and source media before proceeding with any swap
Use clean, well-lit, front-facing reference images of the target identity for the best quality results
Verify the output platform allows synthetic media and add disclosure labels when required by the platform
anti_patterns: Do not process face swaps for public figures or private individuals without confirmed consent
Do not create defamatory, sexually explicit, or otherwise harmful synthetic media regardless of CLI capabilities
Do not skip consent verification even if the CLI tool accepts the inputs without complaint

faq.question: What is face-swap?
faq.answer: A skill that routes face replacement requests to the best AI model on RunComfy based on whether you need still image or video output.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a RunComfy account and the runcomfy CLI installed and authenticated to use this skill.

faq.question: Can I swap faces in videos?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports video face swaps using Wan 2-2 Animate for audio-driven animation and Kling Motion Control Pro for motion transfer.

faq.question: Is face swapping legal?
faq.answer: Face swapping is legal when you have consent from the person whose face is used and rights to the source media. Always check your local laws and platform policies.

faq.question: How many images can I process at once?
faq.answer: Nano Banana Edit supports up to 20 images per batch call. Other routes like GPT Image 2 Edit support up to 10 reference images per call.

faq.question: What should I do if the face swap result looks unnatural?
faq.answer: Try using a higher quality reference image with even lighting and a front-facing pose. You can also switch to GPT Image 2 Edit for more precise multi-reference composition.
